phases.name: Verify Frontend Structure
phases.description: The diagnosis reports 0 TSX files despite frontend: 'yes' and dashboard components existing. Investigate web/dashboard structure to confirm React components are properly structured. Check if files are .jsx instead of .tsx, or if Glob scan missed the files. Verify src/features/ contains expected components (auth, jobs, devices, documents, dashboard, settings). Confirm TypeScript configuration is correct and all components are discoverable. Update scanning logic if needed to properly count frontend artifacts.
success_criteria: All React components located and counted correctly
TSX/JSX files documented in project structure

phases.name: API Documentation with OpenAPI
phases.description: Generate comprehensive OpenAPI/Swagger documentation for all microservices. Create internal/swagger/docs.go with annotations for each endpoint. Document request/response schemas, authentication requirements, error codes, and example payloads. Add swagger-ui middleware to each service for interactive API exploration at /docs endpoint. Include API versioning information. Ensure documentation covers auth-service, registry-service, job-service, storage-service, notification-service, gateway, analytics-service, and organization-service endpoints.
success_criteria: Each service has /docs endpoint with interactive API explorer
OpenAPI spec exported for all services

phases.name: CI/CD Pipeline Setup
phases.description: Create GitHub Actions workflow in .github/workflows/ci.yml for automated testing and deployment. Pipeline should: 1) Run go test ./... with coverage on every push, 2) Execute npm run type-check and npm run test for dashboard, 3) Build all Docker images, 4) Run integration tests against docker-compose services, 5) Deploy to staging on main branch merge. Add separate .github/workflows/release.yml for production releases with semantic versioning. Include status checks for branch protection. Configure caching for Go modules and npm dependencies.
success_criteria: CI runs on all pull requests

phases.name: End-to-End Testing with Playwright
phases.description: Implement comprehensive E2E tests using Playwright for the dashboard frontend. Create tests/e2e/ directory covering: user authentication flows (login, logout, password reset), job management (create, list, cancel, retry jobs), device management (register printers, view status, configure settings), document upload and retrieval, dashboard navigation, and settings changes. Configure Playwright to run against local docker-compose stack and remote environments. Add visual regression testing for critical pages. Integrate E2E tests into CI pipeline with video recordings on failure.
success_criteria: E2E tests cover all major user flows

phases.name: Performance and Load Testing
phases.description: Create load testing suite using k6 or Vegeta to validate system performance under stress. Define test scenarios simulating: concurrent user logins, parallel job submissions, high-frequency agent heartbeats, document upload bursts, and WebSocket connection scaling. Establish performance baselines and SLOs (e.g., 95th percentile latency < 200ms for API calls, 1000 concurrent users). Add tests/load/ directory with scenarios for each service. Create performance dashboard showing metrics over time. Set up automated performance regression detection in CI.
success_criteria: Load tests for each service defined

phases.name: Observability with Prometheus/Grafana
phases.description: Implement comprehensive observability stack with Prometheus metrics and Grafana dashboards. Add prometheus client to each Go service exposing /metrics endpoint with custom metrics for: request latency, error rates, active connections, queue sizes, and business metrics (jobs processed, documents stored). Create Grafana dashboards for: service health, request throughput, database performance, Redis operations, and business KPIs. Add alerting rules for critical conditions (high error rate, service down, queue backlog). Deploy monitoring stack via docker-compose with persistent storage. Implement distributed tracing with Jaeger integration.

phases.name: Multi-Tenancy Deepening
phases.description: Enhance multi-tenancy support across all services. Implement row-level security in PostgreSQL for data isolation. Add organization_id filtering to all queries in repository layers. Create tenant context middleware that extracts organization from JWT and propagates through request context. Add tenant provisioning workflows for new organizations. Implement resource quotas per tenant (max printers, storage limits, job throughput). Add admin panel for organization management. Ensure all services respect tenant boundaries in APIs and background jobs. Document tenant isolation guarantees.

strengths: Frontend components created across 6 feature areas
API Gateway routing to all services

rationale: Project is in excellent health with all core services implemented, tests passing, and build working. The 0 TSX file count is a data discrepancy to verify first. With foundations solid, next phases focus on production readiness: API documentation for developer experience, CI/CD for deployment automation, E2E testing for user confidence, performance validation for scalability, observability for operations, and multi-tenancy deepening for enterprise use. This ordering builds external interfaces (docs, CI/CD) before adding operational complexity (monitoring, multi-tenancy).
